decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 500 to 750 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak temperature at the chip center.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
Ensure the new conductivity value remains physically realistic for the selected material class.
